front.title: Agentic Workflows
front.subtitle: Shifting from single-prompt responses to iterative, multi-step reasoning cycles.

back.text:
An agentic workflow represents a paradigm shift in how Large Language Models are utilized. Instead of generating a final output in a single pass, the AI follows an iterative process of planning, executing, and self-reflecting. This scaffolded approach allows the system to break down complex goals into manageable sub-tasks, much like a human professional managing a project. By using tools and checking its own work, the agent can correct errors and refine its logic before presenting a result.

These workflows often employ specific patterns such as reflection, tool use, and multi-agent collaboration. In a reflection pattern, the model critiques its own draft to improve quality. In tool use, the model leverages external software or APIs to gather real-time data or perform calculations. This structured harness transforms a static model into a dynamic participant capable of solving sophisticated, open-ended problems that require persistent logic and verification.
